overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenant may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may have greater freedom to compete after leaving the system

overrides.override_summary: Oklahoma choice of law may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchise law may apply instead of Oklahoma law

overrides.override_summary: General release at renewal/transfer excludes California Franchise Investment Law claims per Corp Code 31512
overrides.why_it_matters: Release cannot waive California statutory franchise protections

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under Civil Code 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Accelerated royalty provisions may not be enforceable

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate limited to 10% annually
overrides.why_it_matters: FA's 1.5%/month (18% annual) late payment rate is capped at 10%

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction/venue outside Illinois is void for litigation (not arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees can litigate in Illinois rather than Oklahoma

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law controls if inconsistent with FA; Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act supersedes
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchise protections apply regardless of Oklahoma choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Termination and non-renewal provisions may not be enforceable under Sections 19/20 of Illinois FDA
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois may require greater protections against termination

overrides.override_summary: Initial Franchise Fee deferred until franchisor completes all training/initial obligations and franchisee opens
overrides.why_it_matters: IFF is not due until franchisor performs — reduces risk of paying for unfulfilled obligations

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to unilaterally terminate without material violation and not in bad faith (IC 23-2-2.7-1(7))
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protection against arbitrary termination

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require prospective general release of claims under Indiana Deceptive Franchise Practices Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves right to bring statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete limited to within Territory only (not 50-mile radius from all Cookie Advantage businesses)
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly narrower geographic scope than standard FA

overrides.override_summary: Franchise law disputes in Indiana; other disputes in Oklahoma
overrides.why_it_matters: Indiana franchisees can litigate franchise law claims locally

overrides.override_summary: Indiana law applies to franchise law disputes
overrides.why_it_matters: Indiana franchise protections apply

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of Iowa Business Opportunity Promotions Law rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Iowa statutory protections cannot be waived

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland statutory claims preserved at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: May bring lawsuit in Maryland for Maryland Franchise Law claims. 3-year statute of limitations from grant date
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate locally; longer statute than FA's 1-year limit

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations (required by MD Securities Commissioner based on financial condition)
overrides.why_it_matters: Fee deferral specifically triggered by franchisor's financial condition — a signal of regulatory concern

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause (failure to comply with lawful provision + written notice + 30 days to cure)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan provides strongest termination protections

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration/litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees never have to travel to Oklahoma for disputes

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse renewal without fairly compensating for FMV of inventory/equipment/fixtures (if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under different mark)
overrides.why_it_matters: 5-year FA term potentially triggers this protection

overrides.override_summary: No litigation outside Minnesota. Cannot abrogate any MN Chapter 80C rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees can litigate locally with full statutory protections

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60-day cure); 180 days notice of non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer notice/cure periods than FA's 3-20 day windows

overrides.override_summary: No general release permitted (MN Rule 2860.4400(D))
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot be required to sign release at renewal or transfer in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: No jury trial waiver; no liquidated damages; no termination penalties (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Accelerated royalty provision may not be enforceable; right to jury trial preserved

overrides.override_summary: NSF/returned check fee capped at $30 per occurrence (MN Statute 604.113)
overrides.why_it_matters: FA's $100 NSF fee reduced to $30 in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Non-waiver of NY General Business Law Article 33 rights at renewal/transfer
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law protections preserved

overrides.override_summary: General release may not be enforceable under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Release cannot waive statutory franchise protections

overrides.override_summary: Dispute resolution outside ND may not be enforceable under Section 51-19-09
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may have right to litigate locally

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law may not be enforceable under Section 51-19-09
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law may apply instead of Oklahoma law

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages, termination penalties, jury trial waiver, punitive damages waiver may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Multiple damage-limiting provisions potentially voided

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete covenants may be subject to ND Century Code Section 9-08-06
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete may be limited or unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: Franchise fee deferred until all initial obligations fulfilled and franchisee commences business
overrides.why_it_matters: Fee deferral reduces pre-opening financial risk

overrides.override_summary: Venue/law restrictions void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims per Section 19-28.1-14
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate locally under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Initial Franchise Fee deferred until pre-opening obligations complete and franchise open (required by SD Dept of Labor and Regulation based on financial condition)
overrides.why_it_matters: Like Maryland, fee deferral specifically triggered by financial condition concerns

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause per VA Retail Franchising Act Section 13.1-564; unlawful to use undue influence to induce surrender of rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protection against arbitrary termination and coercive practices

overrides.override_summary: Special risk added: investment range ($92,550-$171,250) exceeds franchisor's stockholders' equity ($78,724)
overrides.why_it_matters: Regulatory flag that franchisor's financial capacity is limited relative to required investment

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in Washington
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees never have to travel to Oklahoma for disputes

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning under $100K/year and independent contractors under $250K/year (RCW 49.62). Cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Given that most franchisee employees likely earn under $100K, noncompete is effectively voided for many individuals

overrides.override_summary: Release may not include WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Robust protection of WA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees must reflect reasonable estimated/actual costs. Provisions limiting statute of limitations or rights to jury trial may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer fee ($2,900) may be challenged if it exceeds actual costs

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law Chapter 135 supersedes any inconsistent FA provision
overrides.why_it_matters: WI Fair Dealership Law provides strong franchisee protections on termination and non-renewal
